DSDS Rich Text Definition

A text field that supports multiple content formats. Used across all DSDS schema modules for any field that contains human-written prose.

Source: common/rich-text.schema.json

richText

A text field that supports multiple content formats. Accepts either a plain string (interpreted as markdown for backward compatibility) or a structured object with an explicit format declaration.

Accepts one of:

  • string — A string value. When provided as a bare string, the format is assumed to be 'markdown' for backward compatibility with DSDS documents.
  • object — A structured content object with an explicit format.
    • PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
    valuestringrequiredThe text content.
    format"plain" | "markdown" | "html"requiredThe format of the content. 'plain' is unformatted text. 'markdown' is CommonMark (0.27 minimum). 'html' is sanitized HTML.
↑ Back to top

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification
